2606.27785.title: Output-Space Allocation Costs for Calibration-Guided LLM Compression: An Empirical Study
2606.27785.paper_summary: Training-free compression methods for large language models (LLMs) often use calibration data to guide compression decisions. ROCKET, a recent method combining sparse-dictionary factorization with multi-choice knapsack problem (MCKP) allocation, derives its per-layer factorization from an output reconstruction objective but uses weight-space Frobenius error as the MCKP allocation cost. We investigate whether aligning the allocation cost with the output-space objective improves compressed model fidelity. On Qwen3-8B at 50\% compression, our ROCKET-ActCost achieves +0.8 percentage points higher average accuracy across 8 zero-shot benchmarks (53.1\% vs 52.3\%), but increases WikiText perplexity by 16\% (61.46 vs 52.98). This accuracy-perplexity tradeoff reveals that different allocation objectives favor different downstream metrics. The high correlation ($>$0.99) between weight-space and output-space errors limits allocation divergence, explaining the modest effect size. On Llama-3.2-1B at 20\% compression, the two methods produce near-identical results (53.3\% vs 53.5\% accuracy, 14.45 vs 14.66 PPL), suggesting that the effect of the cost function is minor at lower compression ratios.
2606.27785.paper_summary_zh: 訓練無需的壓縮方法對於大型語言模型（LLMs）通常使用校準數據來指導壓縮決策。ROCKET是一種最近的方法，結合了稀疏字典分解與多選背包問題（MCKP）分配，從輸出重建目標推導其每層的分解，但使用權重空間的弗羅貝尼烏斯誤差作為MCKP的分配成本。我們調查了將分配成本與輸出空間目標對齊是否能改善壓縮模型的保真度。在Qwen3-8B以50%的壓縮率下，我們的ROCKET-ActCost在8個零樣本基準測試中實現了平均準確率提高0.8個百分點（53.1%對52.3%），但WikiText的困惑度增加了16%（61.46對52.98）。這種準確率與困惑度的權衡顯示，不同的分配目標有利於不同的下游指標。權重空間與輸出空間誤差之間的高相關性（$>$0.99）限制了分配的差異，解釋了效果的適度大小。在Llama-3.2-1B以20%的壓縮率下，這兩種方法產生了幾乎相同的結果（準確率53.3%對53.5%，困惑度14.45對14.66），這表明在較低的壓縮比下，成本函數的影響較小。
2606.27785.author: Qiong Tang et.al.
2606.27785.authors: Qiong Tang, Xiangkun Hu, Xiangyang Liu, Yiran Chen, Yunfan Shao
